name: ČSFD Rating
description: Looks up a movie/series on ČSFD (Czech-Slovak Film Database) by name and year during a metadata refresh and adds its percentage rating as a "ČSFD: NN%" tag, visible on the item's detail page in the Jellyfin UI. Requires a valid csfd.cz session cookie configured in the plugin's settings, because csfd.cz is protected by the Anubis anti-bot challenge - see the plugin README.
overview: Shows the ČSFD rating (in %) for movies and series.

versions.changelog: Fix: search results now prefer the result whose year matches the item's production year instead of always taking the first result (ČSFD often lists several unrelated titles sharing a name). Also adds much more detailed Info-level logging (search URL, resolved link, found rating or reason it wasn't set, whether the tag was added) for diagnosing future issues directly from the Jellyfin log.

Fix: implement IHasItemChangeMonitor so a plain Scan Library picks up items that never got a ČSFD rating (or whose cached rating expired) without needing a Replace all metadata refresh. Custom metadata providers are otherwise skipped for already-imported items unless they report a change themselves.

Change: store the ČSFD rating as a "ČSFD: NN%" tag instead of CriticRating. CriticRating is a single shared field other rating plugins (e.g. Rotten Tomatoes/OMDb) also write to, so whichever provider ran last silently won and the ČSFD value never reliably showed up. Tags are additive and display as plain text on the item detail page.

Fix: build against Jellyfin.Controller/Jellyfin.Model 10.11.6 to match the target server's ABI exactly. The 1.0.0.0 build (against 10.11.11) caused a NotSupported/TypeLoadException on 10.11.6 servers due to a binary incompatibility between patch versions.

Initial release: ČSFD rating lookup for movies and series via CriticRating. Note: this build targeted Jellyfin.Controller/Jellyfin.Model 10.11.11 and causes a NotSupported/TypeLoadException on 10.11.6 servers - use 1.0.0.1 instead.
